heading: Oregon
label: 117 Oregon
text: Oregon constitutes one judicial district.Court shall be held at Coquille, Eugene or Springfield, Klamath Falls, Medford, Pendleton, and Portland.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on title 28, U.S.C., 1940 ed., § 183 (, ; , ).Mar. 3, 1911, ch. 231, § 10236 Stat. 1122Nov. 6, 1945, ch. 44759 Stat. 555Provisions relating to appointment and residence of deputies by the clerk and marshal, and maintenance of offices by said officers, were omitted as covered by sections 541 [see 561], 542 [see 561], and 751 of this title.Changes in arrangement and phraseology were made.
Amendments2000— substituted “Eugene or Springfield” for “Eugene”.Pub. L. 106–5181970— provided for holding court at Coquille.Pub. L. 91–2721950—Act , provided for holding court at Eugene.Aug. 3, 1950
